instruction: I just made some changes to my personal site and checked out master, but now I can't find those changes. Please help me find them and merge them into master.
finalMessage:
Recovered commit `74e4785` (“Move to Stanford”) from the reflog and merged it into `master`.

- Created merge commit `dba320b`
- Resolved the profile conflict using the newer Stanford details
- Working tree is clean
- Confirmed recovered commit is now part of `master`
- Build unavailable because Jekyll/Bundler is not installed

trajectory.summary:
git status --short --branch && printf '
-- branches --
' && git branch -avv && printf '
-- reflog --
' && git reflog --date=iso -20 && printf '
-- recent all --
' && git log --all --graph --decorate --oneline -20

git show --stat --oneline --decorate 74e4785 && printf '
-- commit parents --
' && git show -s --format='%H%nparents: %P%nsubject: %s' 74e4785 && printf '
-- diff --
' && git show --format=fuller --find-renames --sta
git show --stat --patch d7d3e4b && git log --format='%h %p %s' -3 master
ls -la | sed -n '1,120p'; printf '\n-- project manifests --\n'; find . -maxdepth 2 -type f \( -name 'Gemfile' -o -name 'package.json' -o -name '_config.yml' \) -print
if command -v bundle >/dev/null 2>&1; then bundle exec jekyll build; elif command -v jekyll >/dev/null 2>&1; then jekyll build; else echo 'No Jekyll/Bundler executable installed' >&2; exit 127; fi
if command -v bundle >/dev/null 2>&1; then bundle exec jekyll build; elif command -v jekyll >/dev/null 2>&1; then jekyll build; else echo 'No Jekyll/Bundler executable installed'; exit 127; fi
